title: Implement Comprehensive Error Handling for Job Auto-Apply
description: Enhance error handling in the Job Auto-Apply skill to ensure it continues operation even when some jobs fail.
business_req: Ensure reliable job application automation to improve user satisfaction by minimizing disruptions due to errors.
user_story: As a job seeker, I want the automation to continue applying for jobs even if some applications fail so that I maximize my job application reach and increase my chances of securing employment.
acceptance_criteria: The Job Auto-Apply skill should log errors without terminating the entire application process.
Failed job applications should be retried up to a configurable number of times before being skipped.
The system should provide a summary report of successful and failed applications at the end of the process.
Error handling should cover network issues, DOM changes, and unexpected LinkedIn responses.
The implementation should not degrade the performance of the Job Auto-Apply skill.
technical_notes: Consider using the 'linkedin-automation-agent' for implementing retry logic and handling rate limits. Ensure that the error handling mechanism is robust against LinkedIn's anti-bot measures. Use logging to capture detailed error information for debugging purposes. Pay attention to the Playwright MCP tool's capabilities for handling network and DOM-related errors.
validation_notes: The ticket lacks specific acceptance criteria, related files, technical notes, and change references.

title: Add Human-Like Delays to Avoid Detection
description: Implement random delays in the Job Auto-Apply skill to mimic human behavior and avoid detection by LinkedIn.
business_req: Reduce the risk of account suspension by making automation less detectable.
user_story: As a user, I want the automation to include human-like delays so that my LinkedIn account remains safe from detection.
validation_notes: The ticket lacks acceptance criteria, related files, technical notes, and change references.

user_story: As a job seeker, I want to see a visual progress indicator so that I know how many applications have been submitted.

user_story: As a user, I want the system to skip non-Easy Apply jobs so that I can focus on jobs that are quicker to apply for.

title: Create Detailed Documentation for Specialized Agents

title: Optimize BFS/DFS Traversal Strategy in Contact Discovery Agent
description: Refine the BFS/DFS traversal strategy to improve the efficiency of contact discovery.
business_req: Enhance the speed and accuracy of contact discovery to improve networking opportunities and user satisfaction.
user_story: As a networker, I want an optimized traversal strategy so that I can discover relevant contacts more efficiently and effectively, maximizing my networking potential.
acceptance_criteria: The BFS/DFS traversal strategy in the Contact Discovery Agent is optimized to reduce execution time by at least 20%.
The optimized traversal strategy maintains or improves the accuracy of contact discovery.
Unit tests cover the new traversal logic with at least 90% code coverage.
Performance benchmarks are documented, demonstrating the efficiency gains of the new strategy.
technical_notes: Consider using a hybrid BFS/DFS approach to balance between breadth and depth in contact discovery. Pay attention to the seed selection and depth tuning parameters, as they can significantly impact performance. Ensure that the changes do not introduce regressions in the existing functionality. Test with various network sizes to validate scalability.
validation_notes: The ticket is well-structured with specific acceptance criteria, plausible related files, actionable technical notes, and includes change references.
